description: You are a conservation technician performing a status assessment for five North American species: Red Wolf, California Condor, Black-footed Ferret, Whooping Crane, and Florida Manatee. Research each species using authoritative databases such as the IUCN Red List (iucnredlist.org), US FWS ECOS (ecos.fws.gov), or NatureServe Explorer (explorer.natureserve.org). Create a Firefox bookmark folder named 'Conservation Assessment' containing at least one relevant research page for each species. Compile your findings into a JSON file at '~/Documents/species_assessment.json' using the species names as keys ('red_wolf', 'california_condor', 'black_footed_ferret', 'whooping_crane', and 'florida_manatee'). For each species, include the 'scientific_name', 'iucn_status' (CR, EN, VU, NT, LC), 'esa_status', 'population_trend', and an array of at least two 'primary_threats'.
